channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fox News Politics News Letter: Ex-college football coach tapped by governor
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE focuses on millions in migrant hotels billed to US taxpayers as DHS Sec. Noem targets FEMA
item.description: Elon Musk suggested that FEMA, the government's federal disaster relief agency, spent millions on hotels for illegal immigrants just last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he’s in ‘no rush’ to develop Gaza
item.description: “Think of it as a big real estate site, and the United States is going to own it, and will slowly, very slowly, we’re in no rush, in development,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he ordered firing of military academies' Board of Visitors
item.description: President Trump's administration is cutting membership at the Boards of Visitors for U.S. military academies this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing. Much better," Trump told Fox News's Bret Baier in an interview.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge directs Trump administration to comply with order to unfreeze federal grants
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island ordered the Trump administration to comply with his order to unfreeze federal grants, after attorneys general for several Democratic states claimed the directive was not being fulfilled. In a short order Monday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons ex-Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich 5 years after commuting his sentence
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned former Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich, whose 14-year sentence for political corruption charges he commuted during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinian refugees in Syria have a message for Gazans: Don’t leave your land
item.description: Palestinians fled the 1948 Mideast War and took refuge in neighboring Syria. After 77 years, they’re still waiting to go back. They are telling Palestinians in Gaza to stay put.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre and Jawad Rizkallah, MPR News

item.title: Trump fires top US ethics official and head of agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired the federal government’s top ethics official as well as the leader of the agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says Palestinians won’t have the right to return to Gaza under his plan
item.description: President Donald Trump said Palestinians in Gaza would not have a right to return under his plan for U.S. “ownership” of the war-torn territory, contradicting other officials in his administration who’ve sought to argue Trump was only calling for the temporary relocation of its population.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court that Trump helped shape could have the last word on his aggressive executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump will need the Supreme Court, with three justices he appointed, to enable the most aggressive of the many actions he has taken in just the first few weeks of his second White House term.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Elon Musk as 'terrific' during Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump calls federal courts blocking Elon Musk "crazy," and predicted the Kansas City Chiefs would win Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Chiefs for Super Bowl win, praising Mahomes
item.description: “I guess, you have to say that when a quarterback wins as much as he’s won, I have to go with Kansas City,” Trump told Fox News’s Bret Baier.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Richard Gere calls Trump a 'bully and a thug' at awards show
item.description: Hollywood star Richard Gere called President Trump a “bully” and a “thug” in his acceptance speech at the Spanish Academy’s Goya Awards on Saturday night. While accepting his honorary lifetime achievement award, Gere warned of the “slippery slope” toward authoritarianism in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump starts term with positive approval rating in CBS poll
item.description: In the CBS/YouGov poll, 53 percent said that when it comes to “the way Donald Trump is handling his job as president,” they support it.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk dodges DOGE scrutiny while expanding his power in Washington
item.description: Elon Musk has promised transparency as President Donald Trump's leader in making the federal government more efficient. But it hasn't worked out that way so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vought seeks to halt actions at CFPB
item.description: Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought is quickly seeking to crack down on the activities of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), preventing it from drawing down more funding and ordering it to "cease all supervision and examination activity.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: The order explicitly prohibits special government employees and those detailed from outside the department from getting access to the systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, NewsNation

item.title: How Republican skeptics in the Senate got to 'yes' on RFK Jr. and Gabbard
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican skepticism in the Senate of President Donald Trump’sCabinet nominees has been worn down, putting his unconventional choices for some of the most powerful positions in the federal government on the verge of confirmation. Floor votes are expected this week on Robert F.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EEOC exists to fight discrimination. Fired official fears a hobbling under Trump
item.description: Jocelyn Samuels was Trump's pick in 2020 to fill a Democratic seat on the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission. She was fired in January, accused of embracing "radical" ideology.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about presidents and security clearances
item.description: Both former President Joe Biden and President Donald Trump have barred their predecessor access to classified information. But historically, it wasn't like that.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Plan to break up Nevada's biggest agency could bring lower health care costs
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is no fan of big government, and a plan he announced in January's State of the State address takes aim at Nevada's biggest agency, the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker pushes bold legislation to protect homeownership from corporations
item.description: In what's being called a "bold new measure," one Tampa Bay lawmaker wants to put families over corporate elites by making sure local Floridians can stay in their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada highway projects stuck as 90-day review plays out; Brightline effects uncertain
item.description: Nevada is waiting for a sign from the federal government: Can we count on funding or not? "So, we have pulled in a lot of what we are doing," NDOT Director Tracy Larkin Thomason told lawmakers on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
